message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>This research note investigates the optimal size of pools for pooled, COVID-19 testing when positive pools will be followed up by individual tests of pool members. Formulae for the optimum are derived and provided. The analysis indicates that</jats:p>
                <jats:p>
                  <jats:list list-type="bullet">
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>optimal pool sizes are unlikely to exceed about 20 individuals in realistic situations,</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>optimal pool size is influenced by prevalence in the population and the extent to which infection is clustered within the population, and,</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>pools are most efficiently comprised of people with homogeneous risk, with heterogeneity across pools.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                  </jats:list>
                </jats:p>
title: Optimal Size of COVID-19 Testing Pools

reference.unstructured: de Bruijn , (1981), N.G. Asymptotic Methods in Analysis, New York, Dover 27
Weisstein, Eric W. (2020) “Lambert W-Function.” From MathWorld--A Wolfram Web Resource. https://mathworld.wolfram.com/LambertW-Function.html
